spec_match.rationale: The page now demonstrates the exact CSS Conditional 5 syntax: CSS snippets use `@supports at-rule(@name)` and the live JS path calls `CSS.supports('at-rule(@name)')`.

interactive.rationale: The text input, quick-test palette, test button, and clear button let users build a custom support-result table.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (browser, container, layer, name, palette, rule, scope, starting, style, supports, type).

edge_cases.rationale: Empty input now reports an invalid state, and unsupported at-rules are shown as fallback/no-support rows. Malformed complex CSS is still not deeply parsed beyond the at-rule token.

references.rationale: See-also now includes https://chromestatus.com/feature/5110744177836032 and the CSS Conditional Rules Level 5 spec.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 52 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

summary: The playground now uses the correct `at-rule(@name)` syntax in CSS and JS, handles empty input, escapes table output, and includes the required ChromeStatus/spec references.
